mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 200 in the CPIAUCSL series signals central bank inaction, leading to real rate compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite inflationary signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in INDPRO (industrial production) and PAYEMS (payroll gains) signal weakening supply-side dynamics, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term between INDPRO and PAYEMS shows no significant correlation with SPX returns (p > 0.1) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% monthly increase in UNRATE (unemployment rate) signals labor market deterioration, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns during monetary tightening cycles.
failure_condition: SPX returns remain positive in 70%+ of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5% during Fed rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising HOUST (housing starts) indicates strong demand-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST growth fails to predict SPX returns with Rank IC > 0.05 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 5% quarterly decline in GDPC1 (GDP) signals economic contraction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns show no significant negative correlation with GDPC1 declines (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) indicates robust demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST shows no significant predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3% monthly decline in PAYEMS (nonfarm payrolls) signals labor market weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns during high unemployment regimes.
failure_condition: SPX returns remain positive in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS declines occur with UNRATE > 5%.
